english: Sch. 1. also t'ám-me-ba (tham me ba), unconnected, scattered, dispersed. - 2. t'am-t'óm (byed) -pa (tham thom (byed) pa) = °t'úm-pa (°thum pa).
(sometimes t'ém-pa (them pa)) complete, full, almost exclusively used as a pleon. addition to the tens up to hundred.

whole, all; added to the singular number: rgyal-k'áms t'ams-cád (rgyal khams thams cad) the whole empire Glr.; lus t'ams-cád na (lus thams cad na) the whole body aches (opp. to one part of it); bód-kyi zaṅs t'ams-cád (bod kyi zangs thams cad) all the copper of Tibet Glr.; more frq. added to a plural (though usually in the form of the singular number): all (the persons or things), de t'ams-cád (de thams cad), rarely dé-dag t'ams-cád (de dag thams cad), all those; t'ams-cád-kyis so-só-nas (thams cad kyis so so nas) all of them one by one, each.
(= °t'ám-pa? (°tham pa?)), sa (sa), or bye-t'áms-su ǰug-pa (bye thams su 'jug pa) to suffer (a person or beast) to stick fast in the mud, in the sand (?) Glr. 84.

= t'a-ra-t'o-ré (tha ra tho re) (cf. t'ór-ba (thor ba)); *t'ar čós-se dug* (*thar chos se dug*) Ld. sit wide asunder, not too close together! t'ar byed-pa (thar byed pa) Mil. to break to pieces, to smash, to crush.
C. *t'ar-°bág* (*thar bag*), W. *t'á-bag* (*tha bag*) a large plate, dish, platter.

to become free, to be saved, *t'ar gos, or goi* (*thar gos, or go'i*) W. he must become free, las (las) from; to be not hindered or prevented, to get through, to get on, to be able to pass, čú-la (chu la) through the water Mil.; zas mi t'ar (zas mi thar) the food cannot pass through Med.; to be released, acquitted, discharged, *t'ím-na* (*thim na*) C. by a court of justice; t'ár-du ǰúg-pa (thar du 'jug pa) to set at liberty, to acquit, with ts'e (tshe) (col. *tse-t'ár-la táṅ-wa* (*tse thar la tang wa*)) to pardon (a malefactor), to grant him his life, frq., to let live (animals) Mil.; often in a religious sense (with or without rnám-par (rnam par)) to be saved, freed, released, viz. from the transmigration of souls; more frq. the pf. t'ár-pa (thar pa) 1. to be free etc., lam t'ar (lam thar) the road is free, passable 2 sbst. freedom, liberty, happiness, eternal bliss, t'ár-pai rgyur gyur (thar pa'i rgyur gyur) it will be serviceable for (my) liberty; t'ár-(pai) lam (thar (pa'i) lam) the road to happiness (a common expression); t'ar-méd-kyi dmyál-ba (thar med kyi dmyal ba) hell without release. 3. adj. free, t'ár-par °gyúr-ba (thar par gyur ba) to become free, byéd-pa (byed pa) to make free, to liberate, to save; t'ár-sa (thar sa) place of refuge, asylum Thgy.
sometimes for t'a-lí (tha li); t'ál-gyis (thal gyis) v. t'ál-ba (thal ba) II. 3.

sbst. 1. dust (cf. rdul (rdul)), ashes, and similar substances; gog-t'ál (gog thal) ashes; *t'ug-t'ál* (*thug thal*) ('soup-dust') roasted barley-flour C. - t'al-kár (thal kar) a kind of elephant, Cs., perh. the ash-coloured. - t'ál-ču (thal chu) lye. - t'al-čén (thal chen) ashes of the dead; also a sort of light gray earth, representing the former, and used for bedaubing the face in masquerades Mil. - t'al-tág (thal thag) Ld. unleavened bread. - t'al-mdóg (thal mdog) ash-coloured, cinereous. - t'al-pyágs (thal phyags) broom Sch. - t'al-byí (thal byi) the gray or cat-squirrel. - t'al-ts'á (thal tsha) a sort of salt Med. - 2. bya-t'al (bya thal) dung of birds Glr.

vb. (Cs. also °t'ál-ba (°thal ba)) 1. to pass, to pass by, *t'al ča dug* (*thal cha dug*) W. he goes past, he does not come in; *zám-pa t'al ča dug* (*zam pa thal cha dug*), he goes past the bridge, does not pass over it; to miss the mark, of an arrow or ball; rba t'al-t'ál oṅ (rba thal thal ong) the waves flow past Mil. - 2. to go, step, pass beyond, lo lṅá-bču t'ál-nas (lo lnga bcu thal nas) when the age of fifty has been passed Wdn.; *ču-ts'ód yčig ts'á-big t'al* (*chu tshod gcig tsha big thal*) W. a little past one o'clock; sṅo-ba-las t'al-nas dmar-žiṅ (sngo ba las thal nas dmar zhing) Thgy., prob. inclining from blue to red; to be in the advance C.; to project, to be prominent, hence t'al-t'úṅ (thal thung) different lengths, one object projecting beyond another; to play a prominent part, to take the lead W.; t'ál-čes-pa (thal ches pa) to exceed the due measure Sch.; *k'a t'al-wa* (*kha thal wa*) to be forward in speaking, bold. - 3. to go or pass through, brág-la yar t'al mar t'al (brag la yar thal mar thal), and p'ar t'al ts'ur t'ál-du °gró-ba (phar thal tshur thal du 'gro ba) to soar up and down befor' rock, and
